---
title: "Weekly Self-Assessment"
subtitle: "First 10 Minutes of Workshop — Bring this open every Thursday"
format: html
number-sections: false
---

::: {.callout-note}
## How to use this page

This is a **10-minute check-in** to do at the start of every Thursday workshop. Work through it on your own, then share briefly with a neighbor. Be honest — this is for you, not for a grade.
:::

---

## Step 1 — Open your project README (2 min)

Go to your GitHub repository and open your `README.md`. Find your **Expected Timeline** section.

- If you have not written a timeline yet, do that now. Use the template in the [Project Home](index.html).

---

## Step 2 — Reflect on last week (3 min)

Answer the following in a few words or sentences. You can write directly in your README or on paper.

**What did you plan to accomplish last week?**

*(Copy the goal from your timeline here, or recall it.)*

<br>

**What did you actually get done?**

*(Be specific: "I ran a preliminary model" is more useful than "I worked on my project.")*

<br>

**What got in the way, if anything?**

<br>

---

## Step 3 — Where are you right now? (1 min)

Pick one:

::: {.callout-tip}
## Status Check

⬜ 🟢 **On track or ahead** — my timeline is still realistic  
⬜ 🟡 **Slightly behind** — I know what I need to do to catch up  
⬜ 🔴 **Significantly behind** — I need to re-plan or get help

:::

If you selected 🟡 or 🔴: write **one concrete step** you will take this week to get back on track.

*(Example: "I will finish cleaning my data file by Wednesday so I can run a model on Thursday.")*

<br>

---

## Step 4 — Update your timeline if needed (2 min)

If your status is 🟡 or 🔴, revise your Expected Timeline in your README now.

If your status is 🟢, confirm that the **next two weeks** of your timeline are still realistic.

::: {.callout-important}
## One thing I will finish by the end of this week

Write it here (or in your README). Make it specific and achievable in the time you have.

<br><br>

:::

---

## Step 5 — Neighbor check-in (2 min)

Share your status (🟢 / 🟡 / 🔴) and your one goal for the week with the person next to you.

- Offer **one** encouraging word or concrete suggestion.
- You do not need to solve their problems — just listen and respond briefly.

---

::: {.callout-note}
## Why do this every week?

Brief, repeated self-monitoring is one of the most consistent findings in educational research for improving long-term project completion. Checking in weekly — rather than just at the end — helps you catch small problems before they become large ones. This takes 10 minutes. The payoff is substantial.
:::

---

*Return to [Project Home](index.html) · See also: [Progress Presentation Instructions](progress-presentation.html)*
